properties.remark: Delayed report. Multiple vehicle crash due to low visibility and blowing dust, per NMDOT and NMSP. Injuries were reported, official number unknown.

Multiple crashes reported on I-10 between Las Cruces and Deming. I-10 east and westbound lanes closed at milepost 123 due to one of the crashes. Visibilities of less than 1/4 mile reported at Deming ASOS and Las Cruces AWOS.
I-10 eastbound is closed at milepost 85 east of Deming due to blowing dust. Areas have low to zero visibility due blowing dust.
Corrects previous dust storm report from 7 SSE Deming. Relayed from WSMR Met, near zero visibility outside of Deming.
Relayed from WSMR Met, near zero visibility outside of Deming.
*** 3 FATAL *** Dust storm caused near zero visibility on I-10 just west of Lordsburg which caused a large pileup.
I-10 Closed MM81.
HWY 11 Closed from zero visibility MM13 to MM33.
I-10 closed at MM132 for blowing dust.
NM 26 closed from blowing dust. MM 0 to MM44.
I-10 closed along Lordsburg Playa from Blowing Dust, from AZ NM state line to Lordsburg.
Vis 1/4 mile or less and falling.
0.25 mile visibility ASOS station KELP El Paso International Airport.
Dona Ana Flood Commission worker reported 1/4 mile visibility.
US 180 Closed due to blowing dust.
Location Approx. Reported from social media.
Location approximate. Near Zero Visibility reported on social media.
DOT reported low visibility and rotating lane closures. Traffic cams also showed poor visibility and stopped traffic.
NM DOT reported heavy dust with low to zero visibility.
NM 11 closed because of blowing dust.
I-10 and NM 549 both closed because of blowing dust.

US-54 shut down in both directions between MM 85 and 88 with visibility roughly 100 yards due to dust storm.

US-54 shut down in both directions due to a pile-up involving 5 semi-trucks; location approximate.

Visibility less than 1200 feet.

NM 9 closed from NM 136 to Columbus, NM.
Exact location unknown. Social media post of zero visibility in Deming.
Location estimated. Storm driven haboob reported over Columbus with vis less than half mile and not within the worst part of storm.

State police reports near zero visibility at Columbus Port of Entry. NM-9 closed between Columbus and Santa Teresa, NM-11 closed between Columbus and Sunshine.
I-10 EB MP 7 observation site reports visibility less than 1/2 mile. Closure of I-10 eastbound from mile marker 0, Arizona State Line to mile marker 20, Lordsburg.
NWS Meteorologist relay of near-zero visibility along NM-11 at Sunshine, NM. Highway remains closed.
El Paso International ASOS reports 1/4 visibility due to dust storm.
Less than 100 yard vis reported to a WSMR Met.
Near zero visibility reported by law enforcement.
*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** Fatal Wreck occurred at MM93 on I-10.

I-10 Closed from Blowing dust.

Vis around 1/4 mile at NMSU.

Vis of around 1/4 mile near exit 162 on I-10.
Report of near zero visibility between MM 94 and 98 on HWY 9.

0.25 Mile vis near Loop 375 and I-10.
Visibility below a quarter mile at mile marker 99.8 on I-10 due to dust storm.

West Texas Mesonet.
New Mexico State Police reported near zero visibility east of Deming between mile marker 85-98 on I-10. Reports of Dust Storm along I-10 west of Deming as well.
0.25 mile visibility reported at Deming Municipal Airport (KDMN) ASOS.
EM reported structure damage at Granada Shopping Mall. One store had the front wall pushed in and roof collapsed and another business had its roof lifted up and then slammed back down.
Mesonet station WSM28 WSMR Tula.
Minor roof damage and shed damage caused by microburst.
US 70 closed at Ruidoso Downs due to flash flooding.
WSMR personnel report flash flooding along Range Road 7 near the intersection of Range Road 10.
WSMR Station WSM18 at Orogrande Gate reports 4.62 inches of rain in two hours. Verified by radar estimates.
NM 185 at milepost 25 & 30 impassible due to water running over roadways. Crews working to clear road.
Several Low Water Crossing are impassable along NM 185.
Time estimate. Campo Del Sol neighborhood flooded from poor drainage following heavy rainfall.
Heavy rain over NE El Paso caused road flooding at McCombs St and US-54. Several vehicles stranded/stuck on the medium as they attempted to avoid the flooded road.
Intersection of Range Road Route 6 and 9 had water flowing over the road and was impassable.
NMDOT mesonet and cameras indicate near zero visibility. Visibility measured at 0.26 miles.
Sensor at I-10 MP11 reported visibility of .27 miles.
Mesonet station WSM51 Malone.
AWOS station KHMN Holloman AFB AWOS.
Mesonet station WSM07 WSMR Frequency.
Water from heavy rainfall over flowing Hwy 154 west of Rincon.
MP12 Observation of .21 miles visibility.
REPORTED AT DEMING MUNICIPAL AIRPORT AT 642 PM.
MM12 on II-10 west of Lordsburg. Reported visibility of .13 miles.
MM12 on I-10 west of Lordsburg reported visibility of 0.06 miles!.
Flood runoff out of Allie Canyon flowed across Highway 35 to a depth of 1.5 ft.

Time Est. based off radar. EM reported several damaged structures, including a blown off roof from a house, damaged restroom at the golf course, and six snapped power poles, from the T or C Golf Course southward to Broadway Street.
US 180 eastbound and westbound lanes are closed from milepost 150 to 152, 12 miles west of Deming. Water on roadway.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding; Confirmed by social media.
Location estimated. Trained Spotter reported flash flooding along Dyer near US 54. Social media showed flooded Autozone parking lot.
Ambient Weather station at Hawkeye Canyon Ct.
Mesonet station WSM05 San Agustin Pass 1.6 E Organ (WSMR).
For WSMR Meteorology Employee reported running water over Baylor Canyon Rd, south of Organ. Location Approx.
NASA EM reports deep water over NASA Rd. Location Approx.
Social Media post of downed powerlines at Ft. Bliss. Time approximated based on top gust at KBIF.
Social media post in the area showing multiple spots of standing and flowing water on roadways.
Time and location estimated. Posted to our channel on X.
Mesonet station WSM25 WSMR Rita.
Mesonet station WSM28 WSMR Tula.
I-10 Closed because of blowing dust along interstate.
Location Approx. Haboob with vis less than 0.25.
Texas Water Development Board station PGF Fabens.
Reported by NWS Student Observer.
Hail covered the ground up to 4" deep.
6 of water running across Hwy 70, out of Soldier Canyon, in Mescalero.
Multi-car pileup reported along I-10 with minor injuries reported. Poor visibility cited as cause of crash.
Flash flooding on Tularosa Creek above Bridal Falls. Debris-laden flow flooded back yard. Cross Creek Road low water crossing would have been dangerously flooded.
Debris flow off the Trout Fire, out of Pictograph Canyon, across HWY 35. Road was impassable. Radar indicated 1.5 - 2.0 in. rainfall.
Low water crossings flowing along NM-35 south of Camp Thunderbird. NMDOT crews monitoring cleanup response.

Mescal Canyon low water crossing at Turtleback Ave flooded and too dangerous to cross. Discharge from Mescal Canyon caused a 0.65 foot backwater spike on the Rio Grande gauge below EB Dam, upstream of Mescal Canyon. Water levels on the Rio Grande came close to riverside porches in T-or-C but no damage.
Street flooding in the Campo del Sol subdivision in NE El Paso, video on social media.
Video on social media outlet showed flooding on I-10 on ramp near downtown.
Flooding in strip mall parking lot on North Loop north of Horizon Blvd. People playing with boats.
Moderate street flooding on Socorro Road.
Flooding, sand, and debris on South Desert Blvd at the I-10 Anthony on-ramp.
Video shows deep flooding along High Valley Road near the Vado Quarry and the approach to the Vado Roundabout.
I-10 is closed due to flooding at MM 151 and the Vado area; fire crews are unable to get through.
Deep flooding on roads and surrounding homes in vicinity of Las Palmeras Road and Jamaica Lane in Berino. Video posted to social media.
Social media video shows major flash flooding in Vado, with floodwaters from Vado Arroyo breaching an embankment in a settling pond near Cebolla Lane, flooding the neighborhood.
Video shows lingering flooding on I-10 eastbound due to major flooding in the Vado Arroyo, with deep turbulent water running over the Frontage Road.
Severe arroyo flooding on Mauer Drive in Bent. Arroyo bank cut, with edge of embankment now 14 feet closer to a home.
Road and culvert damage in several places along Round Mountain Road in Bent.
Flash flooding along Tularosa Creek above Bridal Falls. Flooding of back yard and into house crawl space, artificial pond washed out. Videos found on social media.
Emergency Manager reported closed road and evacuations due to flooding.
Relayed by ABQ. Flash flooding observed from the Salt Fire Burn Scar stemming from Bear Canyon. See LSR issued by ABQ.
Flash Flooding reported by OEM.
Flooding reported at an apartment building.
El Paso OEM reported flooding at Wyoming and Kansas. Google shows road closure and stopped traffic.
I10 and Piedras impassable from flooding. Traffic is backed up on I10.
Flash flooding caused a wall to collapse.
Collapsed wall from Flash Flooding.
I10 MM12 visibility sensor reports 0.36 miles. Traffic cam shows near zero visibility.
6 foot sink hole reported at 2500 Missouri.
NMDot visibility sensor reported visibility of 0.13 miles due to blowing dust at MP-12 on I-10 west of Lordsburg, NM.
Mesonet station NM009 Lordsburg Playa I10 MP9 (NMDOT) vis down to 0.19 mile.
Flooding reported at Hueco State Parks. Low Water Crossing topped with over 6 inches of water.
Location approx. Mescalero EM reported burn scar flooding with the Seven Springs.
Mesonet station NM004 Lordsburg Playa I10 MP12 (NMDOT) vis below 0.25 mile.
Two large trees uprooted. Time based on radar. Radar estimated winds above 60 knots at location.
Time approx. WSMR Range Road washed out from flooding.
Almost 5 inches of rain reported from multiple people in Dell City. Roads were impassable for awhile. Rain fell at least 1 inch per hour for 4 hours.
Water flowing across State Highway 11 between Mile Markers 1 and 2. Road is passable.
County dispatch reported water around 2 feet deep just north of the Columbus-Palomas Port of Entry.
County dispatch reports deep water across State Highway 9 near mile marker 93.
Public report of high water flowing out of Bear Canyon, burn scar flooding of Cherokee Mobile Village and US-70 in Ruidoso Downs.
Mescalero OEM reported heavy flows coming out of Bear Canyon.
Dona Ana OEM reported fast flowing water at Mercantil and Sentenario Streets as well as just south of this point at Conejo Street.
Dona Ana OEM reported flooding in Rincon.
Water Rescue reported at Dripping Springs Rd and Camino Bonito.
1/4 mile visibility measured at the Deming Airport ASOS.

Multiple sensor reports of visibilities below 0.25 miles along I-10 west of Lordsburg.
TXDOT camera showing flooding at Paisano and Executive.
Video showing water rescues at Mesa and Mesita from popular social media account.
I-10 shut down from flooding.
Significant water flow in the Rincon Arroyo at I-25, confirmed by Dona Ana Flood Commission.
Delayed report: Water flow across Visnaga St due to heavy rainfall. Time estimated based on radar.
Road closure on portions of First Street due to flooding.
Broadcast media reports streets inundated with water.
Radar Est. 2.5 inches rainfall. Significant ponding with water running into structures, including restaurant.
Haboob reducing visibility of 1/8 mi. along I-10 between MM7 and MM12. Visibility of 1/4 mi. widespread from Lordsburg to AZ state line.
I-10 westbound lanes have water running across them, but passable.
Social media posts show water flowing over US54 near the Border Patrol check point.
Mesonet station NM003 Lordsburg Playa I10 MP11 (NMDOT).

Tree limbs down throughout Percha Dam State Park. Delayed report.
NM-185 closed between mile markers 18 and 24 due to flooding.

WSMR SAMS Site Tula WSM28. Comms to station were cut-off before storm arrived, peak gust obs retrieved by WMSR staff afterwards.
WSMR SAMS Site Tula Airstrip WSM43 10m tower bent about 30 degrees by wind, appx 3-4 m from base. Peak gust recorded 73 mph. Time of occurrence taken from peak wind time.

WSMR SAMS Site Tula Airstrip WSM43. Comms to station were cut-off before storm arrived, peak gust obs retrieved by WMSR staff afterwards. Tower was bent by winds. Actual peak gust may have been higher.

Flooded low water crossing for NM35. Road impassable.

Debris caused flooding to approach two houses near Fahey and Royal.
Reports of water rescue for a vehicle washed into an arroyo. Emergency response begun at 8:41PM.
Outflow winds blew over tree and pulled down a powerline leaving the area around the El Pavon Apartments without power.
Location and time approximate. Social Media report of snapped power polls around Monticello as well as a damaged barn.
Location and time approximate. Social Media video showing evidence of mud flows and damage from flash flooding near the town of Monticello.
Hail just under the size of golf balls reported at Hatchet Farms.
Flooding from short arroyos draining from the mesa to the north caused flooding in colonias southeast of Fort Hancock, with water surrounding at least one home on Blackfoot Rd.
Unnamed arroyo flooded across Ross Ave, East El Paso St, and Helms Rd, too deep to safely cross.
Cocorahs observer NM-OT-46. Duration of hail about 7 minutes.
